registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_vid_boost_disable_fuse
registers.description: AFS VID boost feature disable; 0x0=feature enabled, 0x1=feature disabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_no1p5_fuse
registers.description: Disable frequency recovery step using divide-by-1.5. Encoding: 0x0 = recovery includes 1.5x, 0x1 = recovery skips 1.5x

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_ovrdval_fuse
registers.description: Interface override value for freq_throttle_delta_f - magnitude of frequency reduction during throttling request relative to nominal target frequency. Encoding: 0x0 = 1.5x, 0x1 = 2x, 0x2 = 4x, 0x3 = 8x

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_t_fuse
registers.description: Latency of each step in frequency during recovery from throttling. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum latency, 0x7 = maximum latency

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_count_fuse
registers.description: Deterministic lock time duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 0, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 =8 , 0x3 = 16.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_damping_fuse
registers.description: AFC GV ramp rate damping and proportional step size in unconstrained ramp rate mode. Encoding: 0x0 = lowest damping (Err/2), 0x1 = lower damping (Err/4), 0x2 = higher damping (Err/8), 0x3 = highest damping (Err/16).

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_exit_err_fuse
registers.description: AFC GV frequency error exit criteria relative to target count with offset given by gv_target_offset fuse. Encoding: 0x0 = lower error (up to 3 DCO codes), 0x1 = higher error (up to 7 DCO codes).

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_ramp_rate_fuse
registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh_fuse
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable_fuse
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg_fuse
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_async_s1align_freq_fuse
registers.description: Static fuse to set S1-align frequency

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_biu_bgf_bypass_separation_fuse
registers.description: Same functional purpose as FB_PMA_BIU_BGF_SEPARATION_FUSE but for the PLL BYPASS MODE.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_biu_bgf_separation_fuse
registers.description: To define the separation number in ns for the BGF during PLL FUNCTIONAL MODE using this formula:  Separation = Fuse_setting * 60/256.   Example:  value 15 =  15 * 60ns / 256 = 3.5156 ns. These values are only used when BGF mode is used (FB_ASYNC_MODE == 0)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_boot_vid_fuse
registers.description: boot vid for module/l2 if applicable in 2.5mV encoding

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_crystal_freq_fuse
registers.description: crystal speed, used to gen 10us beat rate.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_fivr_tele_en_fuse
registers.description: To control FIVR telemetry sampling and pushing at PMA. It has no effect on the actual FIVR IP

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_sleep_disable_ratio_thresh_fuse
registers.description: Specifies the ratio in 100MHz encodingÂ belowÂ which the sleep feature is disabledÂ to prevent retention issues for single-rail support. Shared ownership with araman1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_voltage_mux_equal_vid_fuse
registers.description: l2_voltage_mux_equal_vid is 11 bit threshold fuse. This is a value is milivolt/volts meaning the number programmed in this fuse will be multiplied by 2.5 mv to come up with a voltage value for the fuse. For example value 11'h000 is 0mv, 11'h001 is 2.5 mV, 11'h104 is 650mV. Programmed to the equalization voltage between the two supplies. The mux is low resistance on each leg, so to limit short circuit current voltage on the variable supply will be ramped to the equalization vid before switching. This is set to the value of the static supply. Reset default is 0x0, but the value is effectively NA because the threshold will prevent any switching when both of these fuses are not programmed

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_lfm_ratio_fuse
registers.description: LFM frequency for HWP DOMA logic in 100Mhz encoding

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_mclk_boot_ratio_fuse
registers.description: mod workpoint boot ratio; Same encoding as mod workpoint. Default 16.67Mhz encoding. Maybe different encoding if mclk_use_raw defeature bit is set in pma

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_core_ia_p0_ratio_avx256
registers.description:
P0 when running AVX256 instructions. This is an absolute ration, not delta.
              That is, any core that runs AVX-256 will not run faster.
              Setting this fuse will force the frequency even if there is no IccMax or power limitations, so use with care.
              Units are: Uint

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_no1p5
registers.description: Disable frequency recovery step using divide-by-1.5. Encoding: 0x0 = recovery includes 1.5x, 0x1 = recovery skips 1.5x

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_no1p5
registers.description: Disable frequency recovery step using divide-by-1.5. Encoding: 0x0 = recovery includes 1.5x, 0x1 = recovery skips 1.5x

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_ovrdval
registers.description: Interface override value for freq_throttle_delta_f - magnitude of frequency reduction during throttling request relative to nominal target frequency. Encoding: 0x0 = 1.5x, 0x1 = 2x, 0x2 = 4x, 0x3 = 8x

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_ovrdval
registers.description: Interface override value for freq_throttle_delta_f - magnitude of frequency reduction during throttling request relative to nominal target frequency. Encoding: 0x0 = 1.5x, 0x1 = 2x, 0x2 = 4x, 0x3 = 8x

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_t
registers.description: Latency of each step in frequency during recovery from throttling. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum latency, 0x7 = maximum latency

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_t
registers.description: Latency of each step in frequency during recovery from throttling. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum latency, 0x7 = maximum latency

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_count
registers.description: Deterministic lock time duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 0, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 =8 , 0x3 = 16.

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_count
registers.description: Deterministic lock time duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 0, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 =8 , 0x3 = 16.

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_damping
registers.description: AFC GV ramp rate damping and proportional step size in unconstrained ramp rate mode. Encoding: 0x0 = lowest damping (Err/2), 0x1 = lower damping (Err/4), 0x2 = higher damping (Err/8), 0x3 = highest damping (Err/16).

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_damping
registers.description: AFC GV ramp rate damping and proportional step size in unconstrained ramp rate mode. Encoding: 0x0 = lowest damping (Err/2), 0x1 = lower damping (Err/4), 0x2 = higher damping (Err/8), 0x3 = highest damping (Err/16).

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_exit_err
registers.description: AFC GV frequency error exit criteria relative to target count with offset given by gv_target_offset fuse. Encoding: 0x0 = lower error (up to 3 DCO codes), 0x1 = higher error (up to 7 DCO codes).

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_exit_err
registers.description: AFC GV frequency error exit criteria relative to target count with offset given by gv_target_offset fuse. Encoding: 0x0 = lower error (up to 3 DCO codes), 0x1 = higher error (up to 7 DCO codes).

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_ramp_rate
registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_ramp_rate
registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_0_ia_p0_ratio_downbin
registers.description:
Determines max ratio for this core by subtracting this value from IA_P0_RATIO.
                This is part of Favored Core Turbo (FCT) feature.
                As this is Turbo feature, the result P0 (IA_P0_RATIO - this fuse) must still be at least P1 (IA_P1_RATIO)

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster1_ccp_ia_p0_ratio_downbin
registers.description:
Determines max ratio for this core by subtracting this value from IA_P0_RATIO.
                This is part of Favored Core Turbo (FCT) feature.
                As this is Turbo feature, the result P0 (IA_P0_RATIO - this fuse) must still be at least P1 (IA_P1_RATIO)

registers.name: fw_fuses_config_tdp_ratio_1
registers.description: This ratio is used as the guaranteed ratio (in place of IA_P1_RATIO) whenever Config TDP level 1 is active. A value of 0 disables this configurable TDP point. If both points are disabled then Config TDP is disabled.

registers.name: fw_fuses_config_tdp_ratio_2
registers.description:
This ratio is used as the guaranteed ratio (in place of IA_P1_RATIO) whenever Config TDP level 2 is active. A value of 0 disables this configurable TDP point. If both points are disabled then Config TDP is disabled.
            Note: Level 2 can be non-zero only if level 1 is.

registers.name: fw_fuses_dlvr_media_vout_to_vin_b_coeff
registers.description: Coefficient B of Vin calculation from Vout over the DLVR, according to the formula: Vin = Vout + B*Vout + C + TEMP(Tref-Tmin)/Tref

registers.name: fw_fuses_dlvr_media_vout_to_vin_temp_coeff
registers.description: Coefficient TEMP of Vin calculation from Vout over the DLVR, according to the formula: Vin = Vout + B*Vout + C + TEMP(Tref-Tmin)/Tref

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cep_voltage_offset
registers.description: This fuse is used for CEP threshold encoding, used to correct non-linearity of DAC. Range of -0.5V to 0.5V

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_min_ratio
registers.description: Minimum ratio for IA domain in 100Mhz. Normal values are 4, 5, 6, 7, or 8. 6bits as used for enforce fixed freq by DFx flows

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio
registers.description: max turbo point in 100 MHz- MAX_TURBO_RATIO

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_1_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_1_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 1st ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_2_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_2_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 2nd ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - 2_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_2_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 2 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_3_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_avx256_delta
registers.description:
Amount to lower P0 when running AVX256 instructions.
                         That is, any core that runs AVX-256 will not run faster than: IA_P0_RATIO - IA_P0_RATIO_AVX256_DELTA.
                         Setting this fuse will force the lower frequency even if there is no IccMax or power limitations, so use with care.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p1_ratio
registers.description: guaranteed point frequency in 100 MHz - Max_Non_Turbo_Ratio

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_pn_ratio
registers.description: most efficient point frequency in 100 MHz - Max_Efficiency_Ratio

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_svid_vr_offset_vid
registers.description:
This fuse determines the VID offset of the IA SVID VR. value 0 means disable. MSB is there just to enable
            writing zero, otherwise it is ignored. 7 LSBs are in VID code for IMVP8. The feature needs to be enabled by STRAP
            before the VID offset takes effect. This definition is copied from CFL.

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_dlvr_media_supported
registers.description:
Allows for specific DLVR to enter in regulation. (1= enable, 0 = disable).
              If this OR result is 0 (disable), the DLVR will be in constant bypass mode (never regulated, might be off).
              Default is 0 (disabled).

registers.name: media_gtfs_fuse_aging_volt_threshold
registers.description:
__NAME:gtfs2cpma_aging_volt_thresh_fuse__@@_PO__
GT RF aging Threshold in U1.7Volts. If GT rail voltage is below this threshold then the aging feature is disabled and if it is above this voltage then the feature is enabled provided RF_AGING_DISABLE is enabled. When the feature is enabled When GT rail voltage goes above RF_AGING_V_THRESHOLD value then it will write '1' to CORE_PMA_CR_TARGET_GT1[30] which in turn will propagate to RF EBBs and enable aging protection of RF bitcell by lowering \"vcc\" of the bitcell thereby reducing aging impact on Vccmin of bitcell.

registers.name: media_gtfs_fuse_sleep_transistor_volt_threshold
registers.description:
__NAME:gtfs2cpma_sleep_transistor_volt_thresh_fuse__@@_PO__
GT L3 Sleep Voltage Threshold in U0.7Volts. Below this voltage, sleep should be disabled. This feature only controls the L3 SSAs. The default value for this fuse should be 0.7V.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_p0_ratio
registers.description:
GT maximal frequency, the GT Unslice P0 ratio, in 50 MHz bins.
                         The P0 for GT Slices is GT_P0_RATIO - GT_P0_RATIO_1SLICE_DELTA, or lower if more slices are active.
                         On GT2 products, it will also push the Unslice down, as GT has a single frequency.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_p1_ratio
registers.description:
Guaranteed non-turbo frequency in 50Mhz.
                         This is an HVM fused value used by pcode and reflected to the driver.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_pn_ratio
registers.description:
Lowest requested p-state can be equal or greater than the real minimum in 50 MHz.
                          This is HVM fused value used by pcode and reflected to the driver

registers.name: devid
registers.description: SetIDValue flow DevID

registers.name: devidl6p1
registers.description:
Device ID Lower Bit 6:0 Port 1 (DEVIDL6P1)
State of bit Lower 7-bits of the Device ID from bit-6 to bit-0 of the PCI Express DIDs.
Note: The upper 9 bits of the Device ID is set through Broadcast ID. The lower 7 bits are pulled through Fuse Pull.

registers.name: devidl6p1
registers.description:
Device ID Lower Bit 6:0 Port 1 (DEVIDL6P1)
State of bit Lower 7-bits of the Device ID from bit-6 to bit-0 of the PCI Express DIDs.
Note: The upper 9 bits of the Device ID is set through Broadcast ID. The lower 7 bits are pulled through Fuse Pull.

registers.name: devidl6p1
registers.description:
Device ID - Lower bit for Port 1 (DEVIDL6P1)
Only bit[6:0] is used for lower 7 bits of the Device ID. Upper 9 bits of the Device ID is delivered through SetID message.

registers.name: devidl6p1
registers.description:
Device ID - Lower bit for Port 1 (DEVIDL6P1)
Only bit[6:0] is used for lower 7 bits of the Device ID. Upper 9 bits of the Device ID is delivered through SetID message.

registers.name: devidl6p2
registers.description:
Device ID Lower Bit 6:0 Port 2 (DEVIDL6P2)
State of bit Lower 7-bits of the Device ID from bit-6 to bit-0 of the PCI Express DIDs.
Note: The upper 9 bits of the Device ID is set through Broadcast ID. The lower 7 bits are pulled through Fuse Pull.

registers.name: devidl6p2
registers.description:

State of bit Lower 7-bits of the Device ID from bit-6 to bit-0 of the PCI Express DIDs.
Note: The upper 9 bits of the Device ID is set through Broadcast ID. The lower 7 bits are pulled through Fuse Pull.

registers.name: devidl6p3
registers.description:
Device ID Lower Bit 6:0 Port 3 (DEVIDL6P3)
State of bit Lower 7-bits of the Device ID from bit-6 to bit-0 of the PCI Express DIDs.
Note: The upper 9 bits of the Device ID is set through Broadcast ID. The lower 7 bits are pulled through Fuse Pull.

registers.name: devidl6p4
registers.description:
Device ID Lower Bit 6:0 Port 4 (DEVIDL6P4)
State of bit Lower 7-bits of the Device ID from bit-6 to bit-0 of the PCI Express DIDs.
Note: The upper 9 bits of the Device ID is set through Broadcast ID. The lower 7 bits are pulled through Fuse Pull.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_count
registers.description: Deterministic lock time duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 0, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 =8 , 0x3 = 16.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_damping
registers.description: AFC GV ramp rate damping and proportional step size in unconstrained ramp rate mode. Encoding: 0x0 = lowest damping (Err/2), 0x1 = lower damping (Err/4), 0x2 = higher damping (Err/8), 0x3 = highest damping (Err/16).

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_exit_err
registers.description: AFC GV frequency error exit criteria relative to target count with offset given by gv_target_offset fuse. Encoding: 0x0 = lower error (up to 3 DCO codes), 0x1 = higher error (up to 7 DCO codes).

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_ramp_rate
registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fractional_control_int_ratio
registers.description: Integer Feedback Ratio

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_app_0_pll_gv_vid_boost_disable
registers.description: PLL mode FGV VID boost disable. Encoding: 0x0 = boost request enabled, 0x1 = boost request disabled.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_app_0_tllm_en
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode enable; 0x0 = Disable tight loop lock mode; 0x1 = enable tight loop lock mode

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_app_0_tllm_output_gating
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode distribution clock gating control. Encoding: 0x0 = gate clock until final lock (tight-loop feedback and static DCC) or early lock (long-loop feedback or active DCC); 0x1 = gate clock until early lock; 0x2 = reserved; 0x3 = no clock gating during lock acquisition.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_offset_dca_static
registers.description: Duty cycle offset target with DCS enabled; static duty cycle setting with DCS enabled; 0x00=minimum high phase, 0x20=center setting, 0x3F=maximum high phase

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fusa_0_fll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Frequency based FLL lock detector threshold for FuSa defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fusa_0_fusa_unlockerr_cfg
registers.description: FuSa unlock error indicator feature enable and reset condition: 0x0=disabled, 0x1=reset on side_rst, 0x2=reset on PLL disable, 0x3=reset on power-good de-assertion

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fusa_0_pll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Post-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold above which PLL is considered unlocked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_ack_interrupt_latency
registers.description: Interrupted frequency crawl ack pulse width in pre-div refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0 = 4 cycles,  0x1 = 8 cycles, 0x2 = 16 cycles,  0x3 = 32 cycles

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_dn_delta_f
registers.description: Frequency crawl down ratio step size. Encoding: 0x0=0.5 ratio, 0x1=1 ratio, 0x2=2 ratios, 0x3=3 ratios.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_dn_delta_t
registers.description: Freqency crawl down duration between ratio steps in refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0=1 cycle, 0x1=2 cycles, 0x2=4 cycles, 0x3=6 cycles, 0x4=8 cycles, 0x5=16 cycles, 0x6=24 cycles, 0x7=32 cycles, 0x8-0xF=128 cycles.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_up_delta_f
registers.description: Frequency crawl up ratio step size. Encoding: 0x0=0.5 ratio, 0x1=1 ratio, 0x2=2 ratios, 0x3=3 ratios.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_up_delta_t
registers.description: Freqency crawl up duration between ratio steps in refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0=1 cycle, 0x1=2 cycles, 0x2=4 cycles, 0x3=6 cycles, 0x4=8 cycles, 0x5=16 cycles, 0x6=24 cycles, 0x7=32 cycles, 0x8-0xF=128 cycles.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_duration
registers.description: Duration for which fast lock high gain settings are applied. Encoding: 0x0 = until EarlyLock, 0x1 = 5 refclks, 0x2 = 10 refclks, 0x3 = 15 refclks.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_pll_lockthresh_prelock
registers.description: Pre-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold below which PLL is considered locked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for lower ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac_hifreq
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for higher ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_vco_trim_pg
registers.description: VCO RO powergate trim affecting VCO gain: 0x0 = min (6GHz/V), 0xF = max (26GHz/V)

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpi_trim
registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpi_trim_fastlock
registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting for fast lock: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpp_trim
registers.description: Proportional charge pump strength: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpp_trim_fastlock
registers.description: Proportional charge pump current setting for fast lock: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_iref2_nlvcascode
registers.description: Irefgen2 mode to linearize output current v/s VCO virtual supply

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_iref_trim
registers.description: IREF current strength tuning. Encoding: 0x0 = min strength, 0x1F = max strength

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_ssc_control_ssc_cyc_to_peak_m1
registers.description: SSC cycles to peak minus 1; half the number of steps in the modulation period minus 1. Period of modulation is 2*(value+1) multiplied by the step duration (PLL refclk period). eg 0x257 => 2*(599+1) * (1/38.4MHz) = 600 cycles * 52.083ns = 31.25us. Spread magnitude is determined by the fractional ratio step size multiplied by cyc_to_peak_m1 + 1 (see ssc_frac_step for ratio step size).

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_ssc_control_ssc_frac_step
registers.description: Spread Spectrum Clocking: fractional step configuration; fraction of PLL ratio at which to take frequency modulation steps. Frac_step = PLL ratio * % spread * 2^(num frac bits) / cycles_to_peak. Example for 0.46% spread amplitude and 32kHz period with PLL ratio of 800MHz/38.4MHz and a 22-bit fractional ratio: 20.833 * 0.0046 * 2^22 / 600 = 670 or 0x29E (see cyc_to_peak_m1 to calculate spread period).

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_ssc_control_ssc_mode
registers.description: Spread Spectrum Clocking: SSC spread direction; 0x0 = disabled; 0x1 = down spread; 0x2 = center spread ; 0x3 = up spread

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_voltage_mux_en_fuse
registers.description: This is Enable Fuse. If this is set, is required that the Atom Module is controlling the Module VR and not controlling the L2 VR, as controlled by their respective fuses. This may be removed if all signal toggles can be sufficiently blocked via the threshold value.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_voltage_threshold_fuse
registers.description: L2_VOLTAGE_threshold value: 11 bit threshold fuse. This is a value is milivolt/volts meaning the number programmed in this fuse will be multiplied by 2.5 mv to come up with a voltage value for the fuse. For example value 11'h000 is 0mv, 11'h001 is 2.8 mv, 11'h3ff is 5.731 volts. It Programmed to the value that the L2 voltage mux supply must switch between external Vccram supply and Vccmodule supply. It is programmed to the Vmin of the SRAM. Reset default is 0x0, signifying always select the primary supply. To disable the L2 voltage mux switching to always select the alternate supply, a value of 0x7FF can be fused.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_fast_gv_ratio_propagation_fuse

registers.description: Frequency crawl overdamp final step duration in multiples of programmed delta_t wait cyles. Applies only to increasing frequency crawl. Encoding: 0x0=4x, 0x1=8x.

registers.description: LDO 2 compensation resistor configuration. Encoding: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.description: Reserved for LDO CBB usage.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_misc_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for future use

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_shortpath_enable
registers.description: Select shortened output clock path for reduced jitter, bypassing clock throttling feauture. Encoding: 0x0 = normal output clock path, 0x1 = shortened output clock path.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_disable
registers.description: Fast-lock high-gain during lock acquisition feature disable; 0x0=feature enabled, 0x1=feature disabled

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_ldo_vrefsel
registers.description: LDO VREF trim configuration: 0x0 = minimum, 0x9 = maximum, 0xA-0xF = unused

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_pvd_mode
registers.description: Static Post-VCO Divider setting; 0x0 = div-1; 0x1 = div-2; 0x2 = div-4; 0x3 = div-8

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_pvd_threshold
registers.description: Dynamic (ratio based) Post-VCO Divider setting; 0x0 = use static setting from pvd_mode fuse; 0x1 - 0x3F = binary value of ratio at and below which div-2/4/8 is enabled

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac_hifreq_thresh
registers.description: Output clock frequency threshold above which to use 'highfreq' startup rdac setting. Encoding: 0x1 = 1.6GHz, 0x1 = 2.0GHz.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_ldo_vrefgen_sel
registers.description: LDO Vref source selection; 0x0=external BG-based VREF (bgvref_a), 0x1=internal RDAC-based Vref from vccana_ehv

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_pwrup_clk_ovrd
registers.description: Power-up clock select interface strap override. Encoding: 0x0 = no override, 0x1 = bypass clock, 0x2 = reference clock, 0x3 = legacy RO clock.

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_reserved3
registers.description: Reserved for future use

registers.name: dlvrpll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_ro_freq_sel
registers.description: Contention clearing ring oscillator frequency select. Encoding: 0x0 = fastest (100-800MHz), 0x1 = faster (80-400MHz), 0x2 = slower (50-280MHz), 0x3 = slowest (40-200MHz) (across 0.65V-1.1V, rcss-rcff)

registers.name: endebug_delay_start_key_generation
registers.description: This fuse is used to delay the endebug session by one second .

registers.name: espi_freq_divby8_en
registers.description:
EDS: Not for production use; only intended for use in Engineering Samples (ES).
Enables low frequency div-by-8 mode for all Slaves connected on the eSPI bus. Programmed frequency configuration settings are not affected when this mode is enabled (bus frequency is reduced proportionally relative to programmed setting).
This mode can be optionally overriden (disabled) by asserting the espi_freq_divby8_ovrd soft-strap.

registers.name: fw_fuses_boot_voltage_guardband
registers.description:
Defines early reset voltage guardband, before BIOS can program VR load-line and so.
            Also required for survivability, as patch was not loaded at this point.
            It cannot be a fixed boot voltage as in legacy products, as VR topology is unknown at fusing,
            and as boot freq is vary.
            The recommended value is around 100mV, sufficient to cover for load-line and survivability,
            but still should be within the normal dynamic voltage of all products.

registers.name: fw_fuses_flex_ratio_disable
registers.description: FLEX Ratio Disable

registers.name: fw_fuses_hwp_ee_turbo_disable
registers.description: HWP EE turbo disable, reset value for POWER_CTL.EE_TURBO_DISABLE

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_ddr_power_offset
registers.description: offset for calculating maximum power of DDR for DRAM (VDD2H, VDD2L,VDD1) Components

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_ddr_power_slope
registers.description: slope for calculating maximum power of DDR for DRAM (VDD2H, VDD2L,VDD1) Components

registers.name: fw_fuses_mc0_power_gating_disable
registers.description:
ADL-B0 new behavior: This fuse disables MC0 reset and power gating. They are coupled.
                If MC0 slice is not occupied pcode will logicaly reset it and electrically power gate it, unless this fuse is set.

registers.name: fw_fuses_min_power_of_sku
registers.description:
Min power allowed when setting the power limit control for any time windows in package and
                IA domain level RAPL. This does not affect GT domain level power limiting. 1 Watt
                resolution. Up to 127 Watts. 0x00 means no power limits.

registers.name: fw_fuses_pkgc_disable_mask
registers.description:
HAS: docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/LNL/HAS/PkgC/PkgC.html#fuses
              bit 0 is set: disable C6.1, bit 1 is set: disable C6.2, bit 2 is set: disable C10.1, bit 3 is set: disable C10.2, bit 4 is set: disable C10.3.
              Mask can disable multiple PkgC states

registers.name: fw_fuses_pkgtyp
registers.description: CPU Package Type. Reported in CAPID PKGTYP field.

registers.name: fw_fuses_soc_other_max_power
registers.description:
Maximum power of all components in the SOC that are not calculated or managed
                by other fuses and / or measurements. It is expected that this fuse is populated with
                a worst-case value that covers the SKU or die.
      
                Components included in this power measurements:
      
                * Pcie power
                * VccST power
                * PCH power
                * SA fixed power

registers.name: fw_fuses_turbo_disable
registers.description: turbo disable

registers.name: fw_fuses_turbo_time_window
registers.description:
Programmable clipping threshold for RAPL limits max time window size.  A value of zero
                (default value) means no clipping (time window will be limited only to 448 seconds, the
                maximum supported by pcode).  The format of this number is a 5-bit unsigned number which
                is an exponent, units are 1/1024 seconds (same as RAPL time window units).  For example a
                value of 10 here means a time window of 2^10 * 1/1024 seconds = 1 second.  A better name
                for this fuse would be RAPL_MAX_TIME_WINDOW.

registers.name: vpupll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fll_proc_0_fll_overclock_mode
registers.description: FLL overclocking (OC) mode. Encoding: 0x0 = no overclocking, 0x1 = ratio overclocking with nominal (0.5-1x) reference clock frequency, 0x2 = BCLK overclocking with elevated (1-3x) reference clock frequency, 0x3 = BCLK overclocking with extreme elevated (3-5x) reference clock frequency and ratio limited to 63.

registers.name: vpupll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_ldo2_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 2 compensation resistor configuration. Encoding: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: vpupll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_ldo_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for LDO CBB usage.

registers.name: vpupll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_misc_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for future use

registers.name: vpupll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_shortpath_enable
registers.description: Select shortened output clock path for reduced jitter, bypassing clock throttling feauture. Encoding: 0x0 = normal output clock path, 0x1 = shortened output clock path.

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_encheckoverrideen
registers.description: used as an enable for en_check value, when 1' the value on frequencyMoniterEn value will be used.

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_freqmonrst
registers.description: reset freq_mon when 1' otherwise it doesn't

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_frequencymonitoren
registers.description: Override en_check value, instead of using the en_check input port value. Valid only when enCheckOverrideEn is 1'

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_marginoverrideen
registers.description: Used as an enable for margin override value, when 1' the value on marginOverrideValue will be used.

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_marginoverridevalue
registers.description: Override the ratio value, instead of using the ratio input port value. Valid only when marginOverrideEn is 1'. All values can be used.

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_reserved
registers.description: FREQ_MON reserved
